properties.remark: FORT STEWART EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR GATE 7 IN FORT STEWART AROUND 501 PM AND REMAINED ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST. POWER LINES WERE REPORTED DOWN. MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. AN OFFICIAL NWS DAMAGE SURVEY IS SCHEDULED FOR THURSDAY 4 FEBRUARY 2016.

FORT STEWART EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR GATE 7 IN FORT STEWART AROUND 501 PM AND REMAINED ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST. POWER LINES WERE REPORTED DOWN. NO OTHER DAMAGE REPORTS HAVE BEEN CONFIRMED AT THIS TIME.

CHATHAM COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT RELAYED A REPORT OF TWO WATERSPOUTS OFF TYBEE ISLAND.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED A TORNADO NEAR RICEBORO ON 9/2/16. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 350 AM JUST NORTH OF BLACKBEARD CREEK. IT IS ESTIMATED THAT THE TORNADO SNAPPED OR UPROOTED A FEW THOUSAND TREES IN THE AREA AND ALSO CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO A FEW STRUCTURES. THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN EF1 ON THE EF-SCALE WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105-110 MPH. THE TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 4.83 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 650 YARDS.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED A TORNADO NEAR SKIDAWAY ISLAND ON 9/2/16. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 512 AM AND MOVED NORTHWARD THROUGH THE RESIDENTIAL AREA KNOWN AS THE LANDINGS. NUMEROUS HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND HUNDREDS OF LARGE PINE AND OAK TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE AN EF1 ON THE EF-SCALE WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS OF 105-110 MPH. THE TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 1.67 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 350 YARDS.
MULTIPLE TREES REPORTED DOWN ON SKIDAWAY ISLAND AND DAMAGE TO AT LEAST 12 TO 15 STRUCTURES. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
MULTIPLE TREES REPORTED DOWN ON SKIDAWAY ISLAND AND STRUCTURE DAMAGE TO AT LEAST ONE RESIDENCE LOCATED IN THE 20 BLOCK OF VILLAGE GREEN CIRCLE. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
